result: I love the hush of a library after the doors close, when the day’s errands settle into a soft, warm hush. The building breathes, floors settling, light thinning, the quiet stretching long as a late summer evening. A lone janitor glides down an aisle with a feather broom, and I hear the whisper of pages turning somewhere in another row. My table catches a spill of sunlight through high windows that never quite reaches the chairs, a pool of gold on dark oak. I open a book not because I must finish it, but because the texture of it is a promise—paper, ink, a spine that has traveled further than my feet. My pen scratches like a small bird. I sip coffee, its steam fogging the margin, and think how curiosity is a patient friend. There is a child on a stool somewhere, legs dangling, eyes wide on a diagram of the human heart, memorizing the red rivers. I imagine the same curiosity as a lantern moving through a forest, not to be seen but to see more. When the doors finally open and the world rushes back, I carry that glow to a corner, where the day waits for me to make it softer.

The building breathes, floors settling, light thinning, the quiet stretching long as a late summer evening. A lone janitor glides down an aisle with a feather broom, and I hear the whisper of pages turning somewhere in another row. My table catches a spill of sunlight through high windows that never quite reaches the chairs, a pool of gold on dark oak. I open a book not because I must finish it, but because the texture of it is a promise—paper, ink, a spine that has traveled further than my feet. My pen scratches like a small bird. I sip coffee, its steam fogging the margin, and think how curiosity is a patient friend. There is a child on a stool somewhere, legs dangling, eyes wide on a diagram of the human heart, memorizing the red rivers. I imagine the same curiosity as a lantern moving through a forest, not to be seen but to see more. When the doors finally open and the world rushes back, I carry that glow to a corner, where the day waits for me to make it softer.
